title: Seven Core Sections of a Project Initiation Document (PID)
summary: Outlines the seven essential sections that make up a complete Project Initiation Document, providing a clear framework for project governance and stakeholder alignment.
content: The seven steps are: 1) Provide the Context, 2) Define the Project Parameters, 3) Define the Specifics, 4) Define the Project Breakdown Structure and Resourcing Plan, 5) Define Who’s Who (team & RACI), 6) Identify Risks, Assumptions, Issues, and Dependencies, 7) Share the PID with stakeholders.
can_answer: What sections should be included in a PID?
How can I structure a Project Initiation Document?
warnings: Omitting any of the seven sections can lead to gaps in project governance.
